metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either name and briefly describe the two child tasks in the plan body (e.g., 'Child task A: create shared reference file; Child task B: update code-review tests criterion'), or rephrase the AC to reference the deliverables directly rather than unnamed child tasks.
findings.finding: The AC 'Both child tasks closed with their changes Verified +1 in Gerrit' references 'child tasks' that are never named, described, or decomposed in the plan body. The plan body instead describes two concrete deliverables (a new shared reference file and a targeted code-review criterion addition), but it never maps these to named child tasks, leaving the AC entity ('child tasks') undefined within the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Both child tasks closed'; plan body: no child task description or names
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Both child tasks closed with their changes Verified +1 in Gerrit' with '[ ] Gerrit change adding examples/claude-skills/shared/<reference-file> is Verified +1 and merged. [ ] Gerrit change updating the code-review tests criterion is Verified +1 and merged.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one seeded recall case per new antipattern (non-gating skip/xfail diff; sleep-only race test diff) to src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml, matching the existing R-* dataset pattern.
findings.finding: Child e8d3 adds two new antipatterns (non-gating test; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only synchronization) to the FLAG list in code-review-tests.md but does not add corresponding recall cases to code-review-tests.eval.yaml — and child 43c9 does not cover this either — leaving eval recall coverage for both new antipatterns owned by no sibling.
findings.location: child e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae: src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md + src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: In child e8d3, extend the eval_spec dataset with two new recall entries: (1) id: R-non-gating-skip, mode: non-gating-test, expect: finding, diff showing a new test decorated with @pytest.mark.skipif on a condition that would hold in CI; (2) id: R-sleep-only-race, mode: unrealistic-fault-injection, expect: finding, diff showing a concurrency test whose only synchronization is time.sleep(). Also add a no-fire case (e.g. a test with strict=True xfail + removal condition) as a FP-guard validation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended filename for the shared test-design reference file (e.g. `test-design-standard.md`) so all three skill load directives agree without executor invention.
findings.finding: The shared reference file's filename is never stated; the executor must invent one ('choose an appropriate filename'), which is a defaultable but unspecified design decision.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to Success Criteria: 'The file shall be named `test-design-standard.md` (or equivalent agreed name stated here).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact insertion point (section name or directive) where rebar-brainstorm should reference the shared test-design file, or explicitly state that rebar-brainstorm loads it via its plan-review gate doc rather than inline.
findings.finding: The injection point for the shared file load in rebar-brainstorm is unspecified; the plan acknowledges the skill 'treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs,' implying no natural TDD section exists, yet the success criterion requires it to load the shared reference at 'the point it incorporates testing.'
findings.location: Context — 'rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs'; Success Criteria line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or supplement AC1 with an in-session-verifiable proxy (e.g. 'changes merged to main with CI green' or 'PRs submitted and CI passing'), reserving Gerrit +1 as a process note rather than a blocking acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: AC1 ('Both child tasks closed with their changes Verified +1 in Gerrit') requires external human reviewer action and is not evaluable in-session via a deterministic command or repo artifact; an autonomous agent cannot satisfy this criterion within a single working session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Both child tasks closed with their changes Verified +1 in Gerrit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make 'no weakening' measurable by stating a diffable invariant, e.g. 'all existing RED-first, held-out-oracle, mutation-check, and no-test-weakening directives in rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4 are preserved verbatim or with only additive changes.'
findings.finding: Success criterion 2 ('The skills' held-out discipline is preserved — no weakening of the held-out oracle model') uses the subjective term 'no weakening,' which lacks an observable, measurable definition an executor or reviewer can deterministically evaluate.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The skills' held-out discipline is preserved (no weakening of the held-out oracle model)'
findings.suggested_fix: Reword to: 'Diff of rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4 shows no removal or softening of the existing RED-first, held-out oracle, mutation-check, refactoring-litmus, or no-test-weakening directives; only additive load-reference lines are present.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs (or tighten the existing Gerrit AC) to explicitly verify: (a) CI lint and prompt-index drift gates pass post-merge, and (b) no held-out oracle language is weakened in any of the three skills.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria lists 'CI gates (lint, prompt-index drift) stay green' and 'The skills' held-out discipline is preserved (no weakening of the held-out oracle model)' as outcome deliverables, but neither has a covering Acceptance Criterion. The only AC verifies Gerrit task closure, which is a proxy for delivery but does not directly gate either of these two named outcomes.
findings.location: Success Criteria (items 2 and 4) vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '[ ] CI lint and prompt-index drift checks pass on the merged commits.' and '[ ] Diff of rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4 shows no removal or softening of held-out oracle, RED-first, or no-test-weakening language.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how rebar-brainstorm satisfies 'loaded by all three skills' — either describe where in rebar-brainstorm the shared reference will be injected, or qualify the SC to 'the two skills with inline TDD sections, plus rebar-brainstorm's plan-review gate reference.'
findings.finding: The Context states rebar-brainstorm 'treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs' (i.e., has no inline TDD section of its own), but the Success Criteria states the shared reference is 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.' For rebar-brainstorm, the 'point each incorporates testing' may not exist within the skill file itself, making the universal 'all three skills' claim potentially unsatisfiable as written or requiring a different — undescribed — integration mechanism for rebar-brainstorm.
findings.location: Context: 'rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs' vs. Success Criteria: 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out path for the code-review criterion modification (e.g., the two new antipatterns are added in a separate versioned block that can be reverted independently, or a documented procedure to disable them if false-positive guards prove insufficient).
findings.finding: The code-review 'tests' criterion is modified to add two new antipatterns, changing behavior that reviewers rely on (code that currently passes could now be flagged). The plan mentions false-positive guards but provides no rollback path or back-out plan if those guards prove insufficient in practice and legitimate reviews begin failing spuriously.
findings.location: Success Criteria / 'The code-review tests criterion gains only the two uncovered antipatterns, each with explicit false-positive guards'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' note to the child task covering the criterion change: the two new antipattern entries should be added in a clearly delimited block so that reverting them (git revert or manual removal) is a one-step operation, and document this in the task's Definition of Done.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or README.md in examples/claude-skills/shared/ (or reference one in the plan) documenting the shared-reference pattern: why it exists, how skills load it, and the expectation that future skills follow the same convention.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern — a shared reference file under examples/claude-skills/shared/ that all three skills must load — but specifies no ADR or convention document explaining where this file lives, how it is loaded, and how future skills should adopt the pattern.
findings.location: Success Criteria / 'One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills'
findings.suggested_fix: Create examples/claude-skills/shared/README.md with three sections: 'Purpose', 'Loading convention' (how a skill references this file), and 'When to extend vs. create new shared files'. Reference this README in the plan's deliverables list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the plan (or child tasks) whether the existing inline TDD prose in rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4 is removed and replaced by a pointer to the shared file, or retained and how duplication/divergence is prevented.
findings.finding: The plan says all three skills will load the shared file 'at the point each incorporates testing,' but does not specify whether the existing inline TDD prose in rebar-debug (Steps 4-5) and rebar-implement (Phase 4) is removed, replaced, or merely supplemented — leaving potential for duplicate or stale inline content.
findings.location: Context / 'rebar-debug (Steps 4-5) and rebar-implement (Phase 4) are strong on RED->GREEN integrity'
findings.suggested_fix: State in each child task's scope: 'Remove existing inline TDD prose at [location] and replace with a single load/include reference to shared/test-design-standard.md, keeping any skill-specific context that is not covered by the shared file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief README or CLAUDE.md note in `examples/claude-skills/shared/` (or update the top-level CLAUDE.md) documenting the shared-file loading convention so future skill authors know to extend the shared reference rather than add per-skill inline prose.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `examples/claude-skills/shared/` directory and a shared-file loading convention for all three skills, but does not call for documenting this new pattern in CLAUDE.md, a README in the shared/ directory, or any ADR — leaving future skill authors without guidance on when/how to add to or reference the shared/ location.
findings.location: Context section / Success Criteria ('One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short `examples/claude-skills/shared/README.md` (3–5 lines) explaining: (1) this directory holds canonical cross-skill guidance, (2) all three skills load these files at the relevant point, (3) new skills should follow the same pattern. Alternatively, add one bullet to the relevant CLAUDE.md section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation step to the Acceptance Criteria — e.g., run each updated skill against a representative sample scenario and confirm the new test-design guidance (tier selection, coverage inventory) appears in its output, or conduct a brief dogfooding review of the two new code-review antipattern guards on real PRs.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism demonstrates that the new shared reference or the code-review additions actually improve outcomes for skill users — the only AC checkpoints are Gerrit approval and CI-green, which are process gates, not value-validation.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line such as: 'Spot-check: execute /rebar-debug and /rebar-implement on a canonical sample bug; confirm shared reference guidance (tier selection, coverage inventory) is present in the generated test plan. Execute the updated code-review tests criterion against one known-antipattern PR and one clean PR; confirm the new checks fire and do not false-positive respectively.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact injection point in rebar-brainstorm where the shared reference will be loaded (e.g., 'add a new `## Test-design guidance` callout inside the completion-summary section, with an explicit `Read examples/claude-skills/shared/<filename>.md` instruction before the user records tickets'), since no native TDD section exists in that skill.
findings.finding: The shared-file 'load' mechanism is underspecified for rebar-brainstorm: the plan's own Context notes that rebar-brainstorm 'treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs' (no inline TDD section exists), yet the success criterion requires the shared reference to be 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.' No injection point is named for brainstorm, leaving the implementer to invent whether to add a new section, where to place the Read instruction, and how to frame it — risking the load not firing or landing in an incoherent position.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing'; Context — 'rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan (or the child task for rebar-brainstorm), state: 'For rebar-brainstorm, add a brief callout after the Understanding gate or before ticket recording — the point where test-design decisions are concretely made — that directs the skill to Read the shared reference before authoring acceptance criteria that involve testing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the shared-file approach in the Context or Approach section (e.g., 'A shared file is preferred over per-skill inline duplication because it provides a single update point and avoids drift between the three skills as the TDD standard evolves').
findings.finding: The plan states a positive rationale for WHAT the approach does but not WHY the shared-file-loaded-by-skills mechanism was chosen over the structurally different alternative of inline-expanding each SKILL.md individually — the 'why it fits' justification (e.g., single update point, no drift between skills, appropriate for LLM-read skill files) is absent.
findings.location: Context section — approach description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the cross-skill `shared/` directory pattern against the established per-skill `references/` subdirectory approach (as used by rebar-janitor), or adopt the per-skill pattern instead; document the rationale for whichever is chosen.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel cross-skill shared directory (`examples/claude-skills/shared/`) without acknowledging or justifying it against the established per-skill multi-file reference pattern already used by rebar-janitor (`references/`, `phases/` subdirs). The simpler alternative — each skill's own `references/` folder or a URL link — is neither considered nor ruled out.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism by which the three SKILL.md files will 'load' the shared reference (e.g., native Claude Code multi-file skill composition, a build/concatenation step, inline `@include` directive, or a prose cross-reference), and verify that mechanism is supported before committing to the shared-file architecture.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the shared file will be 'loaded by all three skills' but never specifies the mechanism by which a SKILL.md file loads an external reference. Claude Code skill files are markdown; no include/import primitive is visible in the codebase, making this an unverified capability assertion.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact loading protocol for the shared reference file: state the prose instruction to insert in each SKILL.md (with the literal relative path from each skill's directory to `shared/<filename>.md`), and name the file so all three skills reference the same artifact.
findings.finding: The plan states the shared reference file is 'loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing' but never specifies the loading mechanism. The existing loading pattern in rebar-debug references `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` via a prose instruction pointing to 'this skill's directory' (a co-located file). The new shared file lives in `examples/claude-skills/shared/` — a different directory — and no protocol is defined for how each SKILL.md will reference it (e.g. what prose instruction is inserted, what relative path is used, or whether an include directive exists). An executor must invent both the file name and the cross-directory reference protocol.
findings.location: Success Criteria §1 ('loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing'); Context section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to the plan or the child task spec: 'Each SKILL.md gains a one-line load instruction at the relevant section, e.g. rebar-debug Phase 2 Step 4 preamble: "Before writing the test, read `../shared/test-design.md` for the test-design standard." The path `../shared/<filename>.md` is relative to the skill's own directory. All three skills must use the identical filename and path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the two new antipatterns in the plan (or a linked child-task spec): for each, provide (a) the precise triggering condition, (b) the concrete false-positive guard / 'do not flag' rule, and (c) a one-line 'litmus' test analogous to the existing ones in code-review-tests.md.
findings.finding: The two new antipatterns the plan adds to the code-review 'tests' criterion — 'non-gating tests' and 'unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync' — are named in the plan but their definitions, triggering conditions, and required false-positive guards are never specified anywhere in the plan text. The executor editing `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md` must invent the full antipattern prose, FP-guard formulations, and 'do not flag' rules from scratch.
findings.location: Context section: 'a minimal, guarded addition to the code-review tests criterion for the two meaningful gaps it does not cover (non-gating tests; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback instruction to each skill's load directive: specify whether the skill should halt and surface an error, or proceed with a named inline default, if the shared reference file cannot be read.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback behavior for a skill that references the shared test-design file but cannot locate it (e.g., the file is missing, the skill is invoked from an unexpected working directory, or a future reorganization renames the path). The existing `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` reference in rebar-debug similarly has no explicit fallback, but that file is co-located and thus less fragile; the new cross-directory reference is more brittle.
findings.location: Success Criteria §1; implied by the 'loaded by all three skills' requirement

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
